violations.text: A blender was observed in the hand-washing sink at the bar. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 06/10/2025

Wood is used in the construction of shelving at the bar (chest freezer cup shelf). Non-food contact surfaces of equipment that are exposed to splash, spillage, or other food soiling or that require frequent cleaning must be constructed of a nonabsorbent and smooth material. New Violation. Correct By: 06/11/2025
The Person in charge did not assure compliance with critical code requirements. New Violation. Correct By: 05/28/2025
The establishment does not have a written procedure for cleaning vomit and diarrhea. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/28/2025

Inspector observed employee put tongs in the hand wash sink in the kitchen. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/28/2025

Raw animal foods were not stored properly to prevent cross-contamination. Inspector observed chicken stored above beef in the walk-in cooler. Except when combined as ingredients, different types of raw animal foods must be separated from each other during storage by using separate equipment or arranging each type of food in equipment so that cross contamination of one type with another is prevented. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/28/2025

The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher was 0 parts per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. PIC called for service. Establishment to sanitize in 3 bay sink. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/28/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (ice machine) was observed with red biofilm. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/28/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (containers, knives, spatulas) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/28/2025
Beef, which was prepared in advance, was not cooled to 70 degrees Fahrenheit in 2 hours and then to 41 degrees Fahrenheit within 4 hours. See disposal. New Violation. Correct By: 05/28/2025

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Per interview of PIC, beef was placed in containers greater than four inches deep. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/28/2025

The presence of flies is not controlled as evidenced by small flying insects. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 05/28/2025
Several foods were observed stored on the floor in the walk-in freezer. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/28/2025
Inspector observed desserts, located in walk-in freezer, uncovered and unprotected from contamination. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/28/2025
Inspector observed several meats in walk-in freezer being stored in shopping bags. PIC informed Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/28/2025

Knives were stored on an unclean surface (in between prep units) between uses. During pauses in food preparation, utensils must be stored on a clean surface or in a clean, protected location. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/28/2025

Wood is used in the construction of cup shelving in the chest freezer at the bar. Non-food contact surfaces of equipment that are exposed to splash, spillage, or other food soiling or that require frequent cleaning must be constructed of a nonabsorbent and smooth material. New Violation. Correct By: 05/28/2025
The following door gaskets were observed torn and in poor repair. 1. Chest freezer (bar) 2. Bar cooler New Violation. Correct By: 06/04/2025
The non-food contact surfaces of the kitchen shelving have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 06/11/2025
Drains in the basement are lacking covers. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 05/28/2025
Inspector observed leak at rinse sink faucet. Plumbing systems to be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 06/11/2025

The floors and walls throughout the facility have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 06/11/2025
The door frame in the basement area is in poor repair, observed wood with incomplete seal. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 06/11/2025

The walk-in freezer has an accumulation of frost buildup. The hoods in the kitchen have an accumulation of grease and soil residue. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 06/11/2025
Mops must be allowed to air dry after use. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 05/28/2025
Bar cooler and prep unit gaskets were observed in poor repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/09/2024
Walk-in freezer was in poor repair, observed with an accumulation of ice buildup. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/09/2024
The Person in charge did not assure compliance with critical code requirements. New Violation. Correct By: 04/25/2024
Establishment is lacking an allergen statement. New Violation. Correct By: 05/09/2024

The establishment does not have a written procedure for cleaning vomit and diarrhea. English Responding to Vomiting and Diarrhea Spanish Responding to Vomiting and Diarrhea Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/25/2024
Handwashing sink in basement was not operable. New Violation. Correct By: 05/09/2024
The handwash sink in the women's restroom is lacking signage. New Violation. Correct By: 05/09/2024

Raw animal foods were not stored properly to prevent cross-contamination. Except when combined as ingredients, different types of raw animal foods must be separated from each other during storage by using separate equipment or arrranging each type of food in equipment so that cross contamination of one type with another is prevented. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/25/2024

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (knives) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/25/2024

Rice, which was prepared in advance, was not cooled to 70 degrees Fahrenheit in 2 hours and then to 41 degrees Fahrenheit within 4 hours. PIC voluntarily disposed of. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/25/2024

Chicken and beans were observed held at 51-76 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Reheated to 165 degrees Fahrenheit. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/25/2024

Rice was observed held at 53 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC voluntarily disposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/25/2024

Rice was observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/25/2024

At the time of inspection, ROP tilapia was thawing with package intact. Reduced oxygen packaging fish that bears a label indicating that it is to be kept frozen until time of use shall be removed from the reduced oxygen environment. Temperature was observed to be over 38 degrees Fahrenheit, PIC voluntarily disposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/25/2024

The establishment is lacking a thermometer to test food temperatures. Food temperature measuring devices shall be provided and be readily accessible for checking cooking, cooling, reheating, and hot/cold holding temperatures of time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. New Violation. Correct By: 05/09/2024
Various foods were uncovered in the walk-in freezer. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/25/2024

Plates are being used as scoops in containers of rice and beef. Utensils used to dispense non-time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods such as sugar and flour must be stored in the food item with their handles above the top of the food within the container. Plates were removed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/25/2024

Utensils in the kitchen are displayed with the lip surface up. Utensils are to be stored so that only the handles are touched by employees and consumers if consumer self-service is provided. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/25/2024
Bar cooler and prep unit door gaskets were observed in poor repair. New Violation. Correct By: 05/09/2024
Walk-in freezer was in poor repair, observed with an accumulation of ice buildup. New Violation. Correct By: 05/09/2024
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the chlorine sanitizing solution for the dishwasher. New Violation. Correct By: 05/09/2024
The prep sink in the basement is lacking an air gap. New Violation. Correct By: 05/09/2024

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the basement area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. New Violation. Correct By: 04/25/2024
